sanskrit:
इदं ज्ञानमुपाश्रित्य मम साधर्म्यमागताः।
सर्गेऽपि नोपजायन्ते प्रलये न व्यथन्ति च।।
transliteration:
idaṃ jñānam upāśritya mama sādharmyam āgatāḥ |
sarge ’pi nopajāyante pralaye na vyathanti ca ||
literal_meaning: Taking refuge in this knowledge, they attain My same nature; they are not born at creation, nor do they suffer at dissolution.
contextual_meaning: When a person internalizes non-dual, gunā-tīta knowledge, the nervous system stops treating impermanence as a threat; the fear-cycle of birth/death is replaced by unshakable equanimity.
psychological_state: Post-traumatic growth: the psyche has metabolized the terror of mortality and now rests in a felt-sense of unbroken being.
trigger_scenario: A survivor who once panicked at every MRI scan now calmly reads ‘no change’ on the oncology report, knowing the body may fade yet the core Self remains untouched.
anchor_text: Know yourself as the changeless witness; no birth or death can touch you.
